title: Review: fix: address P0-P3 review findings from eighth round
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two P1 issues: (1) vault put silently clobbers sibling KV fields, a data-loss footgun that will generate user reports; (2) breaking API removals not signaled in commit message. No security surface exposed, no memory unsafety.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) vaultrs kv2::set exact semantics not verified against live API — the clobber finding (b9z.3) is based on KV v2 documentation; if vaultrs does a read-modify-write internally, the bug doesn't exist. (2) CachingStore thundering herd under heavy concurrent load not directly tested. Would raise confidence if: live Vault integration test covers put-with-field then get-without-field to confirm clobber behavior; property-based testing of json_extract_string_field with arbitrary inputs.
